available: implementation and adversarial fixtures created in this run

background.experience: call-sign continuity, controller transfer, and loss-of-communications procedures
background.attention_bias: issuer continuity, handoff order, and recovery under partial failure
background.counter_bias: rejects a successful login as proof of durable identity authority

readers.thesis: Identity survives migration only when the issuer contract, signing continuity, and client handoffs remain under project control.
observations: The architecture repeatedly separates identity authority from runtime implementation.
uncertainties: Production issuer DNS and certificate custody remain untested.

background.experience: identity reconciliation across conflicting names, records, and custodians
background.attention_bias: stable identifiers, provenance, and relationship graphs
background.counter_bias: rejects platform account IDs as canonical person identity

readers.thesis: The identity layer is an evidence graph linking stable subjects, clients, roles, keys, and issuer history rather than a database of provider accounts.
observations: Provider account identifiers do not preserve durable subject relationships across migrations.
uncertainties: Credential-hash portability across replacement runtimes remains implementation-dependent.

agreements: Fix the issuer URL and prevent request-header-derived issuer mutation.
Export client and role relationships independently of the runtime implementation.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes uninterrupted token verification; Reader B prioritizes durable subject and relationship continuity.
omissions_detected: The first design treated realm export as a complete backup despite documented exclusions.
revisions_after_discussion: Require both database restoration and realm import drills, and record export limitations explicitly.

notes_added: Treat issuer continuity as release authority for identity.
Preserve subject, client, role, and key relationships in a provider-neutral evidence graph.
notes_challenged: A realm JSON export alone constitutes complete identity recovery.

privacy.review: Contains synthetic technical abstractions and explicit corpus limits only.
